description: Routing evals: does the query trigger a working-tree diff review against the rubric? Near-misses route to assess-quality (set the quality bar), review-design (design tradeoffs), diagnose-ci (red pipeline), and ship/pr (commit + push).

queries.query: review my changes before I commit
queries.note: Core ask: review staged + unstaged + untracked against the rubric.

queries.query: review the diff and tell me what's wrong
queries.note: Direct diff review with prioritized findings.

queries.query: look over my staged changes and flag anything risky
queries.note: Staged-only scope; security + correctness dimensions lead.

queries.query: do a code review of what I have locally before I open the PR
queries.note: Pre-PR working-tree review, not a remote PR review.

queries.query: are there any bugs or security issues in my current changes
queries.note: Correctness + security dimensions of the rubric on the local diff.

queries.query: check whether my diff has any scope creep or unrelated edits
did I forget to add tests for the new branch I just wrote
review my uncommitted work against main and prioritize the findings
queries.note: Base-ref scope plus prioritized findings list.

queries.query: go over these untracked files I just created and see if they're sound
queries.note: Untracked files are in scope; read them in full.

queries.query: set the quality bar for this project and define what good looks like
queries.note: Framework / quality-bar definition -- assess-quality, not a single-diff review.

queries.query: what are the five questions every change should be judged against
queries.note: The quality framework itself lives in assess-quality.

queries.query: help me reason about whether this design decision is reversible and DRY
why did the CI pipeline fail on this branch
commit these changes and push them
queries.note: Commit + push is ship, not review.

queries.query: open a pull request with a summary of my commits
queries.note: PR creation -- the pr skill, not a working-tree review.

queries.query: review pull request #42 on GitHub and leave comments
queries.note: Remote PR review with inline comments is out of scope; this reviews the local working tree.
